summary: Disclosing an raped individual’s identity without consent may be punished according to Section 228A of the Indian Penal Code, as it addresses the specific issue of protecting individuals from such disclosures related to serious offenses like rape. This law aims primarily at preserving the confidentiality and dignity of survivors while they navigate legal proceedings.
